name: Arkansas SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits to eligible low-income Arkansas residents through an EBT card.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Arkansas Medicaid (ARKids / Adults)
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage to low-income Arkansans including children through ARKids First and adults through Arkansas Works.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, elderly, disabled, and adults up to 138% FPL through Medicaid expansion
incomeLimitNotes: ARKids A up to 142% FPL; ARKids B up to 211% FPL; adults up to 138% FPL

name: Arkansas Division of Workforce Services - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Arkansas Development Finance Authority (ADFA) - Rental Assistance
description: State housing finance agency providing rental assistance, down payment assistance, and affordable housing programs for low-income Arkansas residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Legal Aid of Arkansas
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Arkansans in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Arkansas residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Center for Arkansas Legal Services (CALS)
description: Free legal aid for low-income residents in central and southern Arkansas covering family law, housing, consumer debt, and public benefits cases.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of central and southern Arkansas counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Arkansas Workforce Centers
description: State workforce development offices providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, and training referrals at locations across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Any Arkansas resident seeking employment or career development services

name: Arkansas LIHEAP - Home Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helps eligible households pay heating and cooling bills and provides weatherization assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Arkansas 211
description: Free, confidential information and referral service connecting Arkansans with local health and human services resources 24 hours a day.
eligibilitySummary: Any Arkansas resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Arkansas Foodbank
description: Feeding America affiliate distributing millions of pounds of food annually through a network of partner agencies across 33 counties in central and southern Arkansas.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across central and southern Arkansas

name: Northwest Arkansas Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate providing food to partner agencies and programs across northwest Arkansas counties including Benton, Washington, Madison, and Carroll.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in northwest Arkansas

name: Food Bank of Northeast Arkansas
description: Feeding America member providing hunger relief through a network of partner agencies serving northeast Arkansas communities.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food in northeast Arkansas

name: Arkansas Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides state-level services and benefits coordination for Arkansas veterans including claims assistance, education benefits, and referrals to federal VA programs.
eligibilitySummary: Arkansas veterans and their families

name: Arkansas Crisis Center
description: Provides 24/7 crisis intervention, suicide prevention, and emotional support through phone and text services for anyone in Arkansas experiencing a crisis.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Arkansas experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Arkansas Division of Child Care and Early Childhood Education
description: Administers childcare vouchers and subsidy programs to help low-income working families afford quality childcare in Arkansas.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under 13 needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income generally at or below 85% of state median income

name: Arkansas Transit Association
description: Coordinates public transportation resources across rural and urban Arkansas including fixed-route buses, demand-response transit, and medical transportation.
eligibilitySummary: Arkansas residents needing public transportation

name: Arkansas Rehabilitation Services
description: Helps Arkansans with disabilities prepare for, find, and maintain employment through vocational rehabilitation, training, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Arkansas residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact their ability to work

name: Arkansas TEA (Transitional Employment Assistance)
description: Temporary cash assistance program for very low-income families with children, combined with work requirements and support services to promote self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TEA payment standard for family size

name: Arkansas Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income households to reduce energy bills and improve comfort and safety.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Arkansas WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, and health referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level
hours: Monday-Friday; hours vary by local clinic

name: Arkansas Department of Education - Adult Education
description: Free adult education services including GED preparation, English language learning, workplace literacy, and basic skills classes across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school seeking basic education or GED

name: Arkansas Access to Justice Commission
description: Coordinates efforts to increase access to civil legal services for low-income and disadvantaged Arkansans through self-help resources and referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Arkansas residents with civil legal needs

name: Arkansas Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program
description: Federally funded rental assistance vouchers administered through local housing authorities helping low-income families afford safe, decent housing.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Generally household income at or below 50% of area median income

name: Little Rock Community Action - Central Arkansas Services
description: Community action agency in Little Rock providing comprehensive assistance including emergency rent and utility help, food pantry, and case management services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of central Arkansas, primarily Pulaski County
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Rock Region METRO - Little Rock Public Transit
description: Public bus system serving the Little Rock metropolitan area including Little Rock, North Little Rock, and surrounding communities with fixed routes and paratransit.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Little Rock metro area; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and Medicare cardholders

name: Northwest Arkansas Economic Development District - TANF Support
description: Provides support services and case management for TANF recipients in northwest Arkansas including job readiness, transportation, and emergency assistance.
eligibilitySummary: TANF recipients and low-income families in northwest Arkansas
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet TANF eligibility guidelines

name: Arkansas Health Insurance Marketplace
description: State health insurance marketplace where Arkansans can shop for and enroll in qualified health plans, often with premium tax credits and cost-sharing reductions.
eligibilitySummary: Arkansans without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL
hours: 24/7 phone and online during open enrollment

name: Arkansas Community Correction - Reentry Services
description: Provides reentry support for formerly incarcerated individuals including employment assistance, housing referrals, and transitional services.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals under community correction supervision or recently released from incarceration

name: Arkansas Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Arkansans to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% of federal poverty level or participation in qualifying program

name: Arkansas Single Parent Scholarship Fund
description: Provides scholarships and supportive services to low-income single parents attending college or vocational training in Arkansas to improve their earning potential.
eligibilitySummary: Single parents enrolled in accredited college or vocational programs in Arkansas
incomeLimitNotes: Must demonstrate financial need

name: Arkansas AR Cares - Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide domestic violence hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for survivors of domestic violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence in Arkansas

name: Fayetteville Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Fayetteville area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Fayetteville area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income for public housing; 50% for vouchers

name: Arkansas Promise Scholarship
description: State lottery-funded scholarship for Arkansas residents attending in-state colleges and universities, covering tuition costs for qualifying students.
eligibilitySummary: Arkansas residents attending eligible in-state institutions with qualifying ACT scores and GPA
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below $75,000 for full award

name: Metropolitan Housing Alliance - Little Rock
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 vouchers to low-income families in the Little Rock metropolitan area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Little Rock area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% AMI for public housing; 50% AMI for vouchers
